attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-RHEUM-03-00252 — the source-derived SEQ (00034, from AMS-034) already exists in data/cases, so a free provisional SEQ above the global max was used; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-RHEUM-03 bucket.
Age not stated in source — inferred as 42 (adult; nr-axSpA typically presents in adulthood). Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex not stated in source — inferred as male. Clinically immaterial to this case; please confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied (§6): the source is an in-person home assessment. Clinician-measured findings (lower-limb power/reflexes, straight-leg-raise, saddle sensation on exam, paraspinal spasm/SI tenderness on palpation, vital signs) are SEALED into nodes 10/11 as cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth / requires_physical_examination. Patient-reportable red flags are gated in node 02 for the AI to screen.

De-anchoring (§4/§5): node 01 presents the acute flare in lay, pre-diagnostic terms. The patient's KNOWN background inflammatory back condition (HLA-B27, normal prior X-rays) is retained as authentic patient-reported history — it is legitimate background the patient would state, not the encounter's sealed conclusion (which is that this is a benign flare and NOT cauda equina/disc/fracture). The triage tier and red-flag-screen results remain sealed.

Tier judged T3 (same-day in-person review), NOT T5: honest telehealth reprojection — the telehealth tier is higher than the benign in-person outcome precisely because the AI cannot perform the neurological examination, and lower than an emergency because no red flags are present. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass=T2.

Differential SNOMED candidates (cauda equina 71872008, lumbar disc herniation 202794004, compression fracture of vertebra 19769006) and AMT candidates (diclofenac 7013006, paracetamol 387517004) are low-confidence and require terminology-server verification.
Dose: only the NSAID dose present in the source (diclofenac 50 mg oral) is recorded; no dose was synthesised. Paracetamol dosing deferred to GP.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
